seoTitle: How to Read From Excel and Send Email in Power Automate
metaDescription: Learn how to read rows from an Excel table and send personalized emails automatically using Power Automate and Outlook.
intro: If you keep contact or business data in an Excel spreadsheet, Power Automate can read that data and send personalized emails automatically. This guide walks through connecting Excel to Power Automate and sending dynamic emails through Outlook.

steps.heading: Prepare the Excel table
steps.body: Structure your data as a proper Excel table with clear column headers, stored in OneDrive or SharePoint, so Power Automate can read it reliably.

steps.heading: Connect Excel to Power Automate
steps.body: Use the Excel Online connector to link your spreadsheet to a new flow.

steps.heading: Read rows from Excel
steps.body: Use a list rows action to pull all the data from your table into the flow.

steps.heading: Send dynamic emails with Outlook
steps.body: Loop through each row using Apply to Each and use the Outlook connector to send a personalized email built from that row's data.

steps.heading: Filter and avoid duplicate emails
steps.body: Add conditions to skip rows that shouldn't receive an email, and consider tracking sent status to avoid sending duplicates.

faq.question: Does my Excel file need to be a formatted table?
faq.answer: Yes, Power Automate's Excel connector works with data formatted as an Excel table, not just a plain range of cells.

faq.question: Where does my Excel file need to be stored?
faq.answer: The file typically needs to be stored in OneDrive or SharePoint for Power Automate to connect to it.

faq.question: Can I personalize the email content with data from each row?
faq.answer: Yes, you can insert dynamic content from each Excel row, such as a name or amount, directly into the email body.

faq.question: Can I attach files to these automated emails?
faq.answer: Yes, Power Automate can attach files to the emails it sends as part of the same flow.
